title: Xtra Dense Irish Lamb Casseroles
description: A garlicky and dense Irish dish featuring grape leaves and agemono-deep-fry, suitable for solo dinner and diabetic-friendly diets.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine lamb, garlic, onion, carrots, celery, salt, and black pepper, and mix well.
In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat and deep-fry the grape leaves until crispy, about 2-3 minutes per side.
In a separate pot, combine tomato paste and chicken broth, and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the tomato paste mixture for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.

In a large casserole dish, create a layer of the lamb mixture, followed by a layer of the grape leaves, and then a layer of the tomato paste mixture, repeating this process until all ingredients are used, ending with a layer of the tomato paste mixture on top.
Cover the casserole dish with aluminum foil and bake for 2 hours.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 30 minutes, or until the top is browned and the casserole is hot and bubbly.
Remove the casserole from the oven and let it rest for 10 minutes before serving.
Cook poultry to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F before serving.
